sources.name: Official MCP Registry
sources.provenanceStrength: namespace-verified metadata; upstream repository/package references when publishers provide them
sources.caveats: The registry is unopinionated and in preview; entries still require independent quality, license, and runtime review.

sources.name: Model Context Protocol reference servers
sources.provenanceStrength: official steering-group repository with package release history and license files
sources.caveats: Reference quality does not automatically imply broad end-user usefulness.

sources.name: Smithery
sources.provenanceStrength: market listing and managed connection metadata
sources.caveats: Authentication and redistribution terms must be checked before automated ingestion.

sources.name: Glama MCP Servers
sources.provenanceStrength: marketplace quality, license, and maintenance signals
sources.caveats: Marketplace grades are discovery signals and require primary-source verification.

sources.name: LobeHub MCP Marketplace
sources.provenanceStrength: large multilingual marketplace with repository links for many entries
sources.caveats: Unvalidated listings and very large reported counts require aggressive deduplication and primary-source checks.

sources.name: OpenAI curated Codex plugins
sources.provenanceStrength: official OpenAI repository with structured marketplace files and per-plugin .codex-plugin/plugin.json manifests
sources.caveats: Availability and authentication policy vary by product and plugin; runtime access must still be tested in the target environment.

sources.name: OpenAI Agent Skills legacy repository
sources.provenanceStrength: official historical source with per-skill license files
sources.caveats: The repository README explicitly redirects current Codex skill and plugin examples to openai/plugins; do not use it as the primary current feed.

sources.name: Anthropic Agent Skills
sources.provenanceStrength: official Anthropic skill repository with SKILL.md sources and Claude plugin marketplace metadata
sources.caveats: Licensing is mixed: most examples are Apache-2.0, while docx/pdf/pptx/xlsx skills are source-available and require separate review.

sources.name: Claude Plugins Community
sources.provenanceStrength: read-only official mirror of community plugins that passed Anthropic automated security scanning and distribution review
sources.caveats: Marketplace approval does not prove license compatibility, usefulness, current upstream behavior, or Agent-OPT Web fitness.

sources.name: skills.sh
sources.provenanceStrength: open multi-agent installer with source repository, content hash, install, and leaderboard signals
sources.caveats: The documented v1 API currently requires an issued API key; use official API access rather than scraping when credentials become available.

sources.name: agentskill.sh
sources.provenanceStrength: open CLI with content SHA tracking, community feedback, and documented static security analysis categories
sources.caveats: Reported scale and security grades require independent sampling and primary-source validation before formal qualification.
